DECLARATION OF (SUPPLEMENTAL) LISA K. PADILLA IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S OPPOS. TO DEFT'S NTC & MTN FOR RELIEF FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SF RENTS
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-20-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT BEREN SHOES, INC'S MOTION FOR MANDATORY RELIEF UNDER SECTION 473(B) FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED; DEFAULT IS VACATED, AND DEFENDANT MAY FILE ITS PROPOSED ANSWER. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS DIRECTED TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF $175 AS REASONABLE COMPENSATION FOR EXPENSES INCURRED IN PROCURING THE DEFAULT. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

Ntc Of Motion By Deft.Relief From Entry Of Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT BEREN SHOES, INCORPORATED'S Motion for Mandatory Relief under Section 473(b)From Entry Of Default And Default Judgment IS GRANTED; DEFAULT IS VACATED, AND DEFENDANT MAY FILE ITS PROPOSED ANSWER. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS DIRECTED TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF $175 AS REASONABLE COMPENSATION FOR EXPENSES INCURRED IN PROCURING THE DEFAULT. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =(301/PJB)
